q: Since there is a rapid increase in the number of vehicles, the city is facing worsening traffic problems. (Use 'lead to')
a: The rapid increase in the number of vehicles is leading to worsening traffic problems.

q: As he was driving fast, he lost control and met with an accident. (Rewrite using cause)
a: Driving fast caused him to lose control and meet with an accident.

q: She fell ill since she ate some stale food. (Use 'result in')
a: Eating some stale food resulted in her falling ill.

q: The company had to shut down because their sales dropped drastically. (Use 'bring about')
a: The drastic drop in sales brought about the company having to shut down.

q: He was scolded by his parents because he performed poorly in the exam. (Use 'lead to')
a: Performing poorly in the exam led to him being scolded by his parents.

q: She was fired from her job as she was often late to work. (Use 'cause')
a: Being often late to work caused her to be fired from her job.

q: The city witnessed chaos and unrest due to the shortage of essential resources. (Use 'result in')
a: Going back on his words brought about him losing his credibility.

q: He lost his credibility since he went back on his words. (Use 'bring about')
a: The late submission of the report wasn't submitted on time, so the manager was unhappy.

q: The report wasn't submitted on time, so the manager was unhappy. (Rewrite using cause)
a: The late submission of the report caused the manager's unhappiness.

q: Because of the heavy rain, the match was cancelled. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: The heavy rain brought about the cancellation of the match.

q: She didn't follow the guidelines; therefore, her application was rejected. (Rewrite using lead to)
a: Not following the guidelines led to the rejection of her application.

q: They ignored safety regulations, which resulted in an accident. (Rewrite using result in)
a: Ignoring safety regulations resulted in an accident.

q: His careless remarks offended the audience. (Rewrite using cause)
a: His careless remarks caused offense among the audience.

q: The company introduced a new incentive scheme, so productivity increased. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: The new incentive scheme brought about increased productivity.

q: Since there were technical errors, the project was delayed. (Rewrite using lead to)
a: Technical errors led to a delay in the project.

q: He didn't explain the concept clearly, which confused the students. (Rewrite using result in)
a: His unclear explanation resulted in student confusion.

q: The new policy was unpopular, and it caused widespread protest. (Rewrite using cause)
a: The unpopular policy caused widespread protest.

q: The roads were icy; therefore, several accidents occurred. (Rewrite using result in)
a: Icy roads resulted in several accidents.

q: She practiced hard for months, so she won the competition. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: Her hard practice brought about her victory.

q: The speaker arrived late, which annoyed the audience. (Rewrite using lead to)
a: The speaker's late arrival led to audience annoyance.

q: There was no coordination between departments, so the event failed. (Rewrite using result in)
a: Poor coordination resulted in the event's failure.

q: Because of high demand, the product sold out quickly. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: High demand brought about a quick sellout.

q: The training program was poorly designed, which caused confusion. (Rewrite using cause)
a: The poor design of the training program caused confusion.

q: He ignored her warnings, so he made a big mistake. (Rewrite using lead to)
a: Ignoring her warnings led to a major mistake.

q: The policy changes helped reduce corruption. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: The policy changes brought about a reduction in corruption.

q: She lost her focus during the test, which resulted in lower marks. (Rewrite using result in)
a: Losing focus during the test resulted in lower marks.

q: The system crash delayed the entire operation. (Rewrite using cause)
a: The system crash caused a delay in the operation.

q: Since they couldn't raise funds, the campaign ended early. (Rewrite using lead to)
a: The failure to raise funds led to the campaign's early end.

q: There were power cuts, so many machines stopped working. (Rewrite using result in)
a: Power cuts resulted in machine failures.

q: The new tax law helped increase government revenue. (Rewrite using bring about)
a: The new tax law brought about an increase in government revenue.
